mechanism_narrative: Sustained low federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy, reducing the cost of capital and boosting corporate earnings expectations, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions, which translates into higher consumer spending and corporate profits, driving S&P 500 forward returns positively over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE falling from COVID peak in 2021
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 fails to show statistically significant positive 60-day returns (p > 0.1) following triggered UNRATE declines over a 5-year period, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales combined with robust housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economic expansion that supports higher corporate revenues and S&P 500 valuations, leading to positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Strong retail sales and housing starts in early 2021
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of instances where both RSAFS and HOUST triggers are met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery indicates a rebound in manufacturing and supply chain activity, leading to increased corporate output and positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings improve.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-stat < 1.5) during periods identified as post-recession recovery when INDPRO growth triggers, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP reflects a healthy and expanding economy, which provides a strong fundamental backdrop for corporate earnings and justifies higher equity valuations, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
regime_hint: any regime with positive GDP growth
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are negative on average during periods of positive GDPC1 growth over any 10-year rolling window, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Rapidly accelerating consumer price inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and can trigger monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances of high CPIAUCSL growth, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) coupled with a falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) signifies a robust labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, driving corporate profits and S&P 500 returns higher over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average growth > 0.2% AND UNRATE 3-month change < -0.2pp
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 5-day returns are negative on average when both PAYEMS and UNRATE triggers are met, this hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: A significant surge in housing starts during a period of low interest rates indicates strong investment and consumer confidence in the economy, which typically precedes broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 performance over a 60-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts strong and FEDFUNDS low in 2021
regime_hint: low interest rate, high growth potential
failure_condition: If the S&P 500's 60-day returns are not significantly positive (p > 0.05) when both HOUST and FEDFUNDS triggers are met in a low interest rate regime, this hypothesis is falsified.
